name: GRANULOSA CELL TUMOR

demographics.Size: average size of 12.5 cm (up to 40 cm) in diameter
demographics.Histo: macrofollicular (multiple cysts resembling follicles), microfollicular (with Call-
demographics.Mean age: 13 years; 80% of GCT occur in first 2 decades; only in 3% in women > 30 years
demographics.Path: solid component of granulosa + theca cells; follicles lined by granulosa cells staining

dominant_finding: iso- to hyperattenuating enhancing solid component
discriminating_features: iso- to hyperattenuating enhancing solid component
intermediate SI of solid component + multiple cystic spaces on T2WI = "spongelike"
hyperintense on T1WI ← hemorrhage within tumor cysts

US: large complex multilocular cystic mass with fluid / blood (most frequently) + solid
thick irregular septations but NO intracystic papillary projections
solid component isoechoic to myometrium with Doppler flow
predominantly solid small heterogeneous hypoechoic mass simulating fibroid (uncommon)
CT: iso- to hyperattenuating enhancing solid component
MR: intermediate SI of solid component + multiple cystic spaces on T2WI = "spongelike"
hyperintense on T1WI ← hemorrhage within tumor cysts
peritoneum-based metastases similar to epithelial neoplasm (rare)

Androgenic effect with virilization extremely rare
clinical.Prognosis: low malignant potential; late recurrence following surgery after > 3 decades
